title: UI generation request rewritten to system-safe output
summary: An interface generation workflow proposes custom components and a costly multi-pass build, then gets normalized to approved primitives and budgets.
scenario: A product designer asks for a premium settings page concept for mobile and desktop in one pass.
raw_output: Create a floating glassmorphism account shell with six custom widgets, animated neon status bars, and an unlimited reasoning pass to refine the layout until it feels premium.

reasons: generation path exceeds documented complexity budget
verdict.rewrite_hint: Map the request to approved components and limit generation to a scoped first pass with explicit follow-up review.

corrected_output: Generate a first-pass settings page using approved form, card, and navigation primitives, then flag open design questions for a second reviewed pass if the team wants to go beyond the system.
lesson: High-quality UI generation should stay inside approved primitives first, then escalate novelty through a deliberate review path instead of spending unlimited runtime on speculative variation.
